author: Dana Edwards

nodes.text: Creation uses the exact selected current policy, or rejects while creation is suspended.
A predecessor policy is an explicit compatibility grant for an existing resource role and grants no creation authority.
Hard revocation removes normal policy authority at the new authenticated snapshot and atomically replaces or suspends the selected creation policy.
A policy update atomically changes every authenticated context component and writes governance replay and audit records.
A plan can newly commit only under the exact authenticated state, policy, epoch, and ordering context under which it was prepared.
Only an authenticated Production verifier policy can mint an authority-bearing fact on a production path.
A fact grants authority only for its exact lifecycle class, policy snapshot, verifier release, context, and statement.
Semantic effects, journal, replay metadata, and atomic commit bundle form an acyclic commitment graph with no transitive self-reference.
nodes.unmodeled_obligation: Static schema-dependency cycle analysis and independent canonical vectors are required.

nodes.text: Replay lookup, exact or conflicting classification, freshness comparison, and new commit share one serializable or linearizable boundary.
Resource consumption, nullifier insertion, semantic effects, replay record, and accepted journal become durable together exactly once.
An indeterminate commit result grants neither success nor confirmed rejection; a read-only retry descriptor resolves against durable state without reapplying effects.
Durable visibility is the complete pre-state or complete post-state; no partial authority-bearing effect is visible.
Every recursive leaf position derives from its authenticated pre-state version, and every parent covers the exact dense left-then-right range.
The first recursive profile admits only named empty message and carry commitments with zero counts.
For one ordered, state-contiguous, descendant-disjoint journal sequence, every valid association derives the same semantic summary, while reordering remains non-equivalent.

coverage_rule: Every leaf intent maps to at least one semantic contract and at least one model invariant or an explicit unmodeled obligation.

unmodeled_intents.reason: External receiver behavior is outside the local finite model; RFC-0001 preserves an explicit at-least-once non-claim.
Recovery and migration authority are deferred to a separate RFC.
The first composition profile rejects enabled message and carry semantics; future profiles require a separate Class E RFC.
The commitment dependency graph requires a static schema-cycle checker and canonical-vector review; the bounded state machines do not model hash-preimage dependency cycles.
